text.title: django-base-model Worked Example
text.summary: Billing service: Customer + Order with soft-delete + uid + protected FK.

list.item: FKs: Order.customer → Customer (must retain history when Customer is soft-deleted)
Customer.email migration: replace `unique=True` with `UniqueConstraint(fields=['email'], condition=Q(deleted_at__isnull=True), name='unique_active_customer_email')`.
Both models add UUIDField uid + a separate unique index on uid (NOT swapping the PK).
Order.customer uses on_delete=PROTECT — deleting an active Customer with Orders fails loudly.
